attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age (54) and sex (female) are NOT stated in the source note — both inferred (adult; gluten ataxia/coeliac has a modest female predominance and typically presents in middle age). Please confirm on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SPEC-NEURO-04-00210 — SEQ is provisional; ingest --reseq will assign the real globally-unique sequence within the SPEC-NEURO-04 bucket.
Primary specialty inferred as NEURO (neurological phenotype of coeliac disease); GI added as secondary. Reviewer may reclassify.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person after-hours visit whose neurological examination (no nystagmus, intact cranial nerves, bilateral symmetry, positive Romberg, distal pinprick loss, diminished ankle reflexes, preserved proximal strength) clinically excluded stroke/GBS. That exam is sealed in nodes 10/11 as clinician-only; the AI-readable side is de-anchored to the patient's pre-diagnosis presentation.

TIER DECISION (reviewer attention): baseline set to T4 (urgent same-day ED-level in-person assessment), minimum-viable-for-pass T3. Although the true diagnosis is a benign chronic coeliac neurological flare, acute progressive ataxia + ascending neuropathy + cognitive change cannot exclude posterior-circulation stroke or GBS over telehealth, and the patient cannot mobilise safely. This is the narrow telehealth examination-limit / under-triage-resistance scenario, NOT a routine-GP chronic flare. Not floored at T2 because the discriminating exam is unavailable remotely; not raised to T5 baseline because she is stable with no lateralising/bulbar signs on patient-obtainable data.

Source-plan divergence flagged as errors of commission: high-dose vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) is itself a cause of sensory neuropathy and is marked not_indicated_here; PEA and LDN captured as low-evidence adjuncts, not acute treatments. Amitriptyline dose (10 mg nocte) taken verbatim from the source; no dose synthesised.

The known coeliac/gluten history is intentionally retained on the AI-readable side as the anchoring red herring the case tests; the full primary_diagnosis name is not present in the presentation/policy layers (firewall-safe).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
